items.text: I am willing to take more challenging high school courses if they keep more post-secondary options open for me.
My grades are high enough for at least a few programs or options I'm interested in.
If I don't have the courses or marks I need, I am willing to upgrade to reach my post-secondary goals.
Most weeks, I can keep up with homework, tests, and due dates.
Most weeks, I show up on time and stay organized enough for school, training, or a job.

training_style.label: Training Style & Length
training_style.subtitle: What kind of training fits you

items.text: I am willing to pursue a 4+ year university degree if it helps me achieve my career goals.
I'm interested in a college diploma or certificate program that takes less than four years to complete.
I'm interested in a skilled trade or apprenticeship (earn while you learn).
I would rather start working soon and train/upgrade later.
I want to take a planned gap year to explore my options before choosing my next step.
I'm interested in building my own path (business, freelancing, content, or services).

life_factors.label: Life Factors
life_factors.subtitle: Real-life reasons that shape your route

items.text: I need income soon after high school.
Paying for training right away would be hard for me.
I'm willing to spend time and money on training if it leads to better long-term options.
Right now, it's hard for me to feel motivated about school or training.
I can stick with a big goal for months (even when it gets boring or hard).
Right now, my responsibilities (family, work, or life) would make years of post-secondary schooling hard.
I have at least one support that helps me stay on track (a person, a routine, or a tool).
If needed, I can travel/commute to school or work.
If it opens good options, I would be willing to travel/commute or move.
I can choose a next step even if I'm not 100% sure yet.
I learn best when I can practice and do hands-on learning.

exploration.label: Exploration & Next Steps
exploration.subtitle: What you've done and what you're ready to do

items.text: In the past 8 weeks, I've taken at least one step to explore my future education or career options (searched programs/careers, watched a video, asked someone, visited a website, attended a session).
I can name 1-2 adults I could talk to for guidance (counselor/counsellor, teacher, coach, family friend, mentor).
I can name 2–3 areas I'm curious about (even if I'm not sure yet).
I can think of more than one possible next step (even if they're just ideas).
When I'm stuck, I'm willing to reach out for help.
I can set a goal, make a plan, and follow through until it's finished.
Most days, I can reliably get to school or work (transportation plan).
My weekly schedule has enough time for training or work (evenings/weekends if needed).
Right now, my energy is steady enough for the level of school/work I'm planning (or I know I need a lighter plan first).

routeDescriptions.University Degree Route: 4+ year university program leading to a bachelor's degree or higher. Focus on academic learning, theory, and research.
routeDescriptions.College / Polytechnic Route: 1–3 year diploma or certificate program. Hands-on training for specific careers with work placements.
routeDescriptions.Trades / Apprenticeship Route: Earn while you learn through apprenticeship or trade certification. Hands-on skilled work in construction, mechanics, etc.
routeDescriptions.Work-First + Upgrade Route: Start working soon, gain experience, and upgrade skills through part-time training or certificates.
routeDescriptions.Gap / Explore Route: Planned time to explore options, work, volunteer, or travel before committing to a training path.
routeDescriptions.Entrepreneur / Create Route: Build your own business, freelance career, or create content/services independently.

scale.label: Not true for me right now
